div.p: To all sons of holy Mother church present and future Patrick of Ryedale sends greeting. Know me to have given and granted, and by this my charter to have made firm, to God and to the Church of Saint Mary at Melrose and the monks in that place serving God, in free, pure, and perpetual alms, for the salvation of my lord, King Alexander, and for my own salvation, and for the souls of my ancestors and successors, twenty acres of the land called ‘Raven’s Fen’, by these boundaries; that is, from the upper end of the Harrow valley, the whole of the little stream upwards as far as the land that William of Ryedale gave as a dower to his wife, Matilda Corbet, and from there upwards towards Whitton as the furrow has been drawn between the said land and ‘Raven’s Fen’, as far as the dyke that the monks built, and from there as the old dyke descends into the Harrow valley. I have given this whole land contained within the said boundaries with all its pertinents to the said monks in return for twenty acres of land, free, released, and quit from every earthly service, custom, and secular demand; to be held, possessed and used as seems best to them; as freely, quitely, fully and honourably as they freely, quitely, fully, and honourably hold and possess any alms. And I and my heirs shall maintain and warrant to the monks the whole said land against all men in respect of all things which can be demanded in return for land. With these witnesses: Walter the bishop of Glasgow, Richard the dean of Tweeddale, William son of John, Thomas of Colville, Richard le Nain, Simon of Grubbit, Walter of Ryedale my son, Ralph of Ryedale, Adam of Chatto, Robert of Barnoldby, Adam of Whitton, Hugh of Newbattle, William my nephew, and many others.
